verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque venissent David et viri ejus in Siceleg die tertia, Amalecitæ impetum fecerant ex parte australi in Siceleg, et percusserant Siceleg, et succenderant eam igni.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֞י בְּבֹ֨א דָוִ֧ד וַאֲנָשָׁ֛יו צִֽקְלַ֖ג בַּיּ֣וֹם הַשְּׁלִישִׁ֑י וַעֲמָלֵקִ֣י פָֽשְׁט֗וּ אֶל־נֶ֙גֶב֙ וְאֶל־צִ֣קְלַ֔ג וַיַּכּוּ֙ אֶת־צִ֣קְלַ֔ג וַיִּשְׂרְפ֥וּ אֹתָ֖הּ בָּאֵֽשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now when David and his men were come to Siceleg on the third day, the Amalecites had made an invasion on the south side upon Siceleg, and had smitten Siceleg, and burnt it with fire,
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin David kolmantena päivänä tuli Ziglagiin miehinensä, olivat Amalekilaiset tulleet etelän puolelta Ziglagiin, lyöneet Ziglagin ja polttaneet sen tulella,
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, when David and his men were come to Ziklag on the third day, that the Amalekites had invaded the south, and Ziklag, and smitten Ziklag, and burned it with fire;

verses.latin_vulgate: Et captivas duxerant mulieres ex ea, a minimo usque ad magnum : et non interfecerant quemquam, sed secum duxerant, et pergebant itinere suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁבּ֨וּ אֶת־הַנָּשִׁ֤ים אֲשֶׁר־בָּהּ֙ מִקָּטֹ֣ן וְעַד־גָּד֔וֹל לֹ֥א הֵמִ֖יתוּ אִ֑ישׁ וַיִּֽנְהֲג֔וּ וַיֵּלְכ֖וּ לְדַרְכָּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And had taken the women captives that were in it, both little and great: and they had not killed any person, but had carried them with them, and went on their way.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja olivat vieneet vaimot, jotka siellä olivat, pienimmästä niin suurimpaan; ei he yhtäkään surmanneet, mutta veivät pois ja menivät tiehensä.
verses.king_james: And had taken the women captives, that were therein: they slew not any, either great or small, but carried them away, and went on their way.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum ergo venissent David et viri ejus ad civitatem, et invenissent eam succensam igni, et uxores suas, et filios suos et filias, ductas esse captivas,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֨א דָוִ֤ד וַאֲנָשָׁיו֙ אֶל־הָעִ֔יר וְהִנֵּ֥ה שְׂרוּפָ֖ה בָּאֵ֑שׁ וּנְשֵׁיהֶ֛ם וּבְנֵיהֶ֥ם וּבְנֹתֵיהֶ֖ם נִשְׁבּֽוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: So when David and his men came to the city, and found it burnt with fire, and that their wives, and their sons, and their daughters, were taken captives,
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin David tuli miehinensä kaupunkiin, katso, se oli tulella poltettu, ja heidän emäntänsä ja poikansa ja tyttärensä viedyt vangiksi;
verses.king_james: So David and his men came to the city, and, behold, it was burned with fire; and their wives, and their sons, and their daughters, were taken captives.

verses.latin_vulgate: levaverunt David et populus qui erat cum eo voces suas, et planxerunt donec deficerent in eis lacrimæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשָּׂ֨א דָוִ֜ד וְהָעָ֧ם אֲשֶׁר־אִתּ֛וֹ אֶת־קוֹלָ֖ם וַיִּבְכּ֑וּ עַ֣ד אֲשֶׁ֧ר אֵין־בָּהֶ֛ם כֹּ֖חַ לִבְכּֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: David and the people that were with him, lifted up their voices, and wept till they had no more tears.
verses.finnish_biblia: Korotti David ja väki, joka hänen kanssansa oli, äänensä ja itkivät siihenasti, ettei heillä enää voimaa itkeä ollut.
verses.king_james: Then David and the people that were with him lifted up their voice and wept, until they had no more power to weep.

verses.latin_vulgate: Siquidem et duæ uxores David captivæ ductæ fuerant, Achinoam Jezrahelites, et Abigail uxor Nabal Carmeli.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּשְׁתֵּ֥י נְשֵׁי־דָוִ֖ד נִשְׁבּ֑וּ אֲחִינֹ֙עַם֙ הַיִּזְרְעֵלִ֔ית וַאֲבִיגַ֕יִל אֵ֖שֶׁת נָבָ֥ל הַֽכַּרְמְלִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: For the two wives also of David were taken captives, Achinoam, the Jezrahelitess, and Abigail, the wife of Nabal of Carmel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Davidin kaksi emäntää olivat myös vankina viedyt pois: Ahinoam Jisreeliläinen ja Abigail Nabalin emäntä Karmelista.
verses.king_james: And David’s two wives were taken captives, Ahinoam the Jezreelitess, and Abigail the wife of Nabal the Carmelite.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et contristatus est David valde : volebat enim eum populus lapidare, quia amara erat anima uniuscujusque viri super filiis suis et filiabus : confortatus est autem David in Domino Deo suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֵּ֨צֶר לְדָוִ֜ד מְאֹ֗ד כִּֽי־אָמְר֤וּ הָעָם֙ לְסׇקְל֔וֹ כִּי־מָ֙רָה֙ נֶ֣פֶשׁ כׇּל־הָעָ֔ם אִ֖ישׁ עַל־בָּנָ֣ו וְעַל־בְּנֹתָ֑יו וַיִּתְחַזֵּ֣ק דָּוִ֔ד בַּיהֹוָ֖ה אֱלֹהָֽיו׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And David was greatly afflicted: for the people had a mind to stone him, for the soul of every man was bitterly grieved for his sons and daughters: but David took courage in the Lord his God.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David oli suuresti ahdistettu, sillä kansa tahtoi hänen kivittää, että kaiken kansan sielut olivat murheissa poikainsa ja tytärtensä tähden. Mutta David vahvisti itsiänsä Herrassa Jumalassansa.
verses.king_james: And David was greatly distressed; for the people spake of stoning him, because the soul of all the people was grieved, every man for his sons and for his daughters: but David encouraged himself in the Lord his God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait ad Abiathar sacerdotem filium Achimelech : Applica ad me ephod. Et applicavit Abiathar ephod ad David.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר דָּוִ֗ד אֶל־אֶבְיָתָ֤ר הַכֹּהֵן֙ בֶּן־אֲחִימֶ֔לֶךְ הַגִּֽישָׁה־נָּ֥א לִ֖י הָאֵפ֑וֹד וַיַּגֵּ֧שׁ אֶבְיָתָ֛ר אֶת־הָאֵפ֖וֹד אֶל־דָּוִֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to Abiathar, the priest, the son of Achimelech: Bring me hither the ephod. And Abiathar brought the ephod to David.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David sanoi papille AbJatarille Ahimelekin pojalle: tuo minulle päällisvaate. Ja AbJatar toi Davidille päällisvaatteen.
verses.king_james: And David said to Abiathar the priest, Ahimelech’s son, I pray thee, bring me hither the ephod. And Abiathar brought thither the ephod to David.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et consuluit David Dominum, dicens : Persequar latrunculos hos, et comprehendam eos, an non ? Dixitque ei Dominus : Persequere : absque dubio enim comprehendes eos, et excuties prædam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁאַ֨ל דָּוִ֤ד בַּֽיהֹוָה֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר אֶרְדֹּ֛ף אַחֲרֵ֥י הַגְּדוּד־הַזֶּ֖ה הַאַשִּׂגֶ֑נּוּ וַיֹּ֤אמֶר לוֹ֙ רְדֹ֔ף כִּֽי־הַשֵּׂ֥ג תַּשִּׂ֖יג וְהַצֵּ֥ל תַּצִּֽיל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David consulted the Lord, saying: Shall I pursue after these robbers, and shall I overtake them, or not? And the Lord said to him: Pursue after them: for thou shalt surely overtake them and recover the prey.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin kysyi David Herralta ja sanoi: pitääkö minun ajaman sitä joukkoa takaa? saanenko minä heidät kiinni? Hän sanoi hänelle: aja heitä takaa, ja sinä totisesti saavutat heidät ja otat saaliin.
verses.king_james: And David enquired at the Lord, saying, Shall I pursue after this troop? shall I overtake them? And he answered him, Pursue: for thou shalt surely overtake them, and without fail recover all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abiit ergo David, ipse et sexcenti viri qui erant cum eo, et venerunt usque ad torrentem Besor : et lassi quidam substiterunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֣לֶךְ דָּוִ֗ד ה֚וּא וְשֵׁשׁ־מֵא֥וֹת אִישׁ֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר אִתּ֔וֹ וַיָּבֹ֖אוּ עַד־נַ֣חַל הַבְּשׂ֑וֹר וְהַנּוֹתָרִ֖ים עָמָֽדוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: So David went, he and the six hundred men that were with him, and they came to the torrent Besor: and some, being weary, stayed there.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin meni David matkaan, hän ja kuusisataa miestä, jotka hänen kanssansa olivat, ja tulivat Besorin ojan tykö; ja muut seisahtivat siinä.
verses.king_james: So David went, he and the six hundred men that were with him, and came to the brook Besor, where those that were left behind stayed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Persecutus est autem David ipse, et quadringenti viri : substiterant enim ducenti, qui lassi transire non poterant torrentem Besor.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּרְדֹּ֣ף דָּוִ֔ד ה֖וּא וְאַרְבַּע־מֵא֣וֹת אִ֑ישׁ וַיַּֽעַמְדוּ֙ מָאתַ֣יִם אִ֔ישׁ אֲשֶׁ֣ר פִּגְּר֔וּ מֵעֲבֹ֖ר אֶת־נַ֥חַל הַבְּשֽׂוֹר׃
verses.douay_rheims: But David pursued, he and four hundred men: for two hundred stayed, who, being weary, could not go over the torrent Besor.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta David ja neljäsataa miestä ajoivat takaa; mutta kaksisataa miestä, jotka siihen seisomaan jäivät, suuttuivat menemästä Besorin ojan ylitse.
verses.king_james: But David pursued, he and four hundred men: for two hundred abode behind, which were so faint that they could not go over the brook Besor.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et invenerunt virum ægyptium in agro, et adduxerunt eum ad David : dederuntque ei panem ut comederet, et biberet aquam,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיִּמְצְא֤וּ אִישׁ־מִצְרִי֙ בַּשָּׂדֶ֔ה וַיִּקְח֥וּ אֹת֖וֹ אֶל־דָּוִ֑ד וַיִּתְּנוּ־ל֥וֹ לֶ֙חֶם֙ וַיֹּ֔אכַל וַיַּשְׁקֻ֖הוּ מָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they found an Egyptian in the field, and brought him to David: and they gave him bread to eat, and water to drink,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he löysivät yhden Egyptin miehen kedolta, sen he veivät Davidin tykö, ja antoivat hänen leipää syödä ja vettä juoda,
verses.king_james: And they found an Egyptian in the field, and brought him to David, and gave him bread, and he did eat; and they made him drink water;

verses.latin_vulgate: sed et fragmen massæ caricarum, et duas ligaturas uvæ passæ. Quæ cum comedisset, reversus est spiritus ejus, et refocillatus est : non enim comederat panem, neque biberat aquam, tribus diebus et tribus noctibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתְּנוּ־לוֹ֩ פֶ֨לַח דְּבֵלָ֜ה וּשְׁנֵ֤י צִמֻּקִים֙ וַיֹּ֔אכַל וַתָּ֥שׇׁב רוּח֖וֹ אֵלָ֑יו כִּ֠י לֹא־אָ֤כַל לֶ֙חֶם֙ וְלֹא־שָׁ֣תָה מַ֔יִם שְׁלֹשָׁ֥ה יָמִ֖ים וּשְׁלֹשָׁ֥ה לֵילֽוֹת׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: As also a piece of a cake of figs, and two bunches of raisins. And when he had eaten them, his spirit returned, and he was refreshed: for he had not eaten bread, nor drunk water, three days and three nights.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja antoivat rypäleen fikunia ja kaksi rypälettä rusinoita; ja sittenkuin hän oli syönyt, tuli hänen henkensä virvoitetuksi, sillä ei hän ollut kolmena päivänä eikä kolmena yönä leipää syönyt eikä vettä juonut.
verses.king_james: And they gave him a piece of a cake of figs, and two clusters of raisins: and when he had eaten, his spirit came again to him: for he had eaten no bread, nor drunk any water, three days and three nights.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit itaque ei David : Cujus es tu ? vel unde ? et quo pergis ? Qui ait : Puer ægyptius ego sum, servus viri Amalecitæ : dereliquit autem me dominus meus, quia ægrotare cœpi nudiustertius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר ל֤וֹ דָוִד֙ לְֽמִי־אַ֔תָּה וְאֵ֥י מִזֶּ֖ה אָ֑תָּה וַיֹּ֜אמֶר נַ֧עַר מִצְרִ֣י אָנֹ֗כִי עֶ֚בֶד לְאִ֣ישׁ עֲמָלֵקִ֔י וַיַּעַזְבֵ֧נִי אֲדֹנִ֛י כִּ֥י חָלִ֖יתִי הַיּ֥וֹם שְׁלֹשָֽׁה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David said to him: To whom dost thou belong; or whence dost thou come? and whither art thou going? He said: I am a young man of Egypt, the servant of an Amalecite: and my master left me, because I began to be sick three days ago.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David sanoi hänelle: kenenkä sinä olet ja kustas olet? Egyptin nuorukainen sanoi: minä olen Amalekilaisen palvelia, ja minun herrani on hyljännyt minun; sillä minä tulin sairaaksi jo kolme päivää sitte.
verses.king_james: And David said unto him, To whom belongest thou? and whence art thou? And he said, I am a young man of Egypt, servant to an Amalekite; and my master left me, because three days agone I fell sick.

verses.latin_vulgate: Siquidem nos erupimus ad australem plagam Cerethi, et contra Judam, et ad meridiem Caleb, et Siceleg succendimus igni.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲנַ֡חְנוּ פָּשַׁ֜טְנוּ נֶ֧גֶב הַכְּרֵתִ֛י וְעַל־אֲשֶׁ֥ר לִיהוּדָ֖ה וְעַל־נֶ֣גֶב כָּלֵ֑ב וְאֶת־צִֽקְלַ֖ג שָׂרַ֥פְנוּ בָאֵֽשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: For we made an invasion on the south side of Cerethi, and upon Juda, and upon the south of Caleb, and we burnt Siceleg with fire.
verses.finnish_biblia: Me ryöstimme etelästä päin Kretin ja Juudan, etelästä päin Kalebin, ja poltimme Ziglagin.
verses.king_james: We made an invasion upon the south of the Cherethites, and upon the coast which belongeth to Judah, and upon the south of Caleb; and we burned Ziklag with fire.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque ei David : Potes me ducere ad cuneum istum ? Qui ait : Jura mihi per Deum quod non occidas me, et non tradas me in manus domini mei, et ego ducam te ad cuneum istum. Et juravit ei David.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר אֵלָיו֙ דָּוִ֔ד הֲתוֹרִדֵ֖נִי אֶל־הַגְּד֣וּד הַזֶּ֑ה וַיֹּ֡אמֶר הִשָּׁ֩בְעָה֩ לִּ֨י בֵאלֹהִ֜ים אִם־תְּמִיתֵ֗נִי וְאִם־תַּסְגִּרֵ֙נִי֙ בְּיַד־אֲדֹנִ֔י וְאוֹרִֽדְךָ֖ אֶל־הַגְּד֥וּד הַזֶּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David said to him: Canst thou bring me to this company? and he said: Swear to me by God, that thou wilt not kill me, nor deliver me into the hands of my master, and I will bring thee to this company. And David swore to him.
verses.finnish_biblia: David sanoi hänelle: vietkös minun sen sotaväen tykö? Hän sanoi: vannos minulle Jumalan kautta, ettes minua tapa etkä anna minua minun herrani käsiin, niin minä vien sinun sen sotaväen tykö.
verses.king_james: And David said to him, Canst thou bring me down to this company? And he said, Swear unto me by God, that thou wilt neither kill me, nor deliver me into the hands of my master, and I will bring thee down to this company.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui cum duxisset eum, ecce illi discumbebant super faciem universæ terræ comedentes et bibentes, et quasi festum celebrantes diem, pro cuncta præda et spoliis quæ ceperant de terra Philisthiim et de terra Juda.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣רִדֵ֔הוּ וְהִנֵּ֥ה נְטֻשִׁ֖ים עַל־פְּנֵ֣י כׇל־הָאָ֑רֶץ אֹכְלִ֤ים וְשֹׁתִים֙ וְחֹ֣גְגִ֔ים בְּכֹל֙ הַשָּׁלָ֣ל הַגָּד֔וֹל אֲשֶׁ֥ר לָקְח֛וּ מֵאֶ֥רֶץ פְּלִשְׁתִּ֖ים וּמֵאֶ֥רֶץ יְהוּדָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when he had brought him, behold they were lying spread abroad upon all the ground, eating and drinking, and as it were keeping a festival day, for all the prey and the spoils which they had taken out of the land of the Philistines, and out of the land of Juda.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän vei hänen; ja katso, he olivat hajoittaneet heitänsä koko maahan, söivät, joivat ja hyppäsivät ilosta kaiken sen suuren saaliin tähden, jonka he olivat saaneet Philistealaisten ja Juudan maalta.
verses.king_james: And when he had brought him down, behold, they were spread abroad upon all the earth, eating and drinking, and dancing, because of all the great spoil that they had taken out of the land of the Philistines, and out of the land of Judah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et percussit eos David a vespere usque ad vesperam alterius diei, et non evasit ex eis quisquam, nisi quadringenti viri adolescentes, qui ascenderant camelos et fugerant.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּכֵּ֥ם דָּוִ֛ד מֵהַנֶּ֥שֶׁף וְעַד־הָעֶ֖רֶב לְמׇחֳרָתָ֑ם וְלֹא־נִמְלַ֤ט מֵהֶם֙ אִ֔ישׁ כִּי֩ אִם־אַרְבַּ֨ע מֵא֧וֹת אִֽישׁ־נַ֛עַר אֲשֶׁר־רָכְב֥וּ עַל־הַגְּמַלִּ֖ים וַיָּנֻֽסוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David slew them from the evening unto the evening of the next day, and there escaped not a man of them, but four hundred young men, who had gotten upon camels, and fled.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David löi heitä aamuhämärästä ruveten ehtooseen asti, liki toiseen päivään; niin ettei heistä yhtäkään päässyt, paitsi neljäsataa nuorukaista, jotka istuivat kamelein päälle ja pakenivat.
verses.king_james: And David smote them from the twilight even unto the evening of the next day: and there escaped not a man of them, save four hundred young men, which rode upon camels, and fled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Eruit ergo David omnia quæ tulerant Amalecitæ, et duas uxores suas eruit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּצֵּ֣ל דָּוִ֔ד אֵ֛ת כׇּל־אֲשֶׁ֥ר לָקְח֖וּ עֲמָלֵ֑ק וְאֶת־שְׁתֵּ֥י נָשָׁ֖יו הִצִּ֥יל דָּוִֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: So David recovered all that the Amalecites had taken, and he rescued his two wives.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin David pelasti kaikki mitä Amalekilaiset ottaneet olivat, ja David pelasti myös kaksi emäntäänsä.
verses.king_james: And David recovered all that the Amalekites had carried away: and David rescued his two wives.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nec defuit quidquam a parvo usque ad magnum, tam de filiis quam de filiabus, et de spoliis, et quæcumque rapuerant : omnia reduxit David.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹ֣א נֶעְדַּר־לָ֠הֶ֠ם מִן־הַקָּטֹ֨ן וְעַד־הַגָּד֜וֹל וְעַד־בָּנִ֤ים וּבָנוֹת֙ וּמִשָּׁלָ֔ל וְעַ֛ד כׇּל־אֲשֶׁ֥ר לָקְח֖וּ לָהֶ֑ם הַכֹּ֖ל הֵשִׁ֥יב דָּוִֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And there was nothing missing small or great, neither of their sons or their daughters, nor of the spoils, and whatsoever they had taken, David recovered all.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ei puuttunut niistä mitään, pientä eli suurta, pojista ja tyttäristä, eli saaliista kun he heillensä ottaneet olivat: David toi ne kaikki takaperin jälleen.
verses.king_james: And there was nothing lacking to them, neither small nor great, neither sons nor daughters, neither spoil, nor any thing that they had taken to them: David recovered all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et tulit universos greges et armenta, et minavit ante faciem suam : dixeruntque : Hæc est præda David.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקַּ֣ח דָּוִ֔ד אֶת־כׇּל־הַצֹּ֖אן וְהַבָּקָ֑ר נָהֲג֗וּ לִפְנֵי֙ הַמִּקְנֶ֣ה הַה֔וּא וַיֹּ֣אמְר֔וּ זֶ֖ה שְׁלַ֥ל דָּוִֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he took all the flocks and the herds, and made them go before him: and they said: This is the prey of David.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David otti kaikki lampaat ja härjät ja antoi ajaa ne karjansa edellä, ja he sanoivat: tämä on Davidin saalis.
verses.king_james: And David took all the flocks and the herds, which they drave before those other cattle, and said, This is David’s spoil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit autem David ad ducentos viros qui lassi substiterant, nec sequi potuerant David, et residere eos jusserat in torrente Besor : qui egressi sunt obviam David et populo qui erat cum eo. Accedens autem David ad populum, salutavit eos pacifice.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֣א דָוִ֗ד אֶל־מָאתַ֨יִם הָאֲנָשִׁ֜ים אֲשֶֽׁר־פִּגְּר֣וּ ׀ מִלֶּ֣כֶת ׀ אַחֲרֵ֣י דָוִ֗ד וַיֹּֽשִׁיבֻם֙ בְּנַ֣חַל הַבְּשׂ֔וֹר וַיֵּֽצְאוּ֙ לִקְרַ֣את דָּוִ֔ד וְלִקְרַ֖את הָעָ֣ם אֲשֶׁר־אִתּ֑וֹ וַיִּגַּ֤שׁ דָּוִד֙ אֶת־הָעָ֔ם וַיִּשְׁאַ֥ל לָהֶ֖ם לְשָׁלֽוֹם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And David came to the two hundred men, who, being weary, had stayed, and were not able to follow David, and he had ordered them to abide at the torrent Besor: and they came out to meet David, and the people that were with him. And David coming to the people, saluted them peaceably.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin David tuli kahdensadan miehen tykö, jotka olivat suuttuneet seuraamasta Davidia ja olivat jääneet Besorin ojan tykö, menivät he Davidia vastaan ja sitä sotaväkeä joka hänen kanssansa oli. Ja David meni sen väen tykö ja tervehti heitä ystävällisesti.
verses.king_james: And David came to the two hundred men, which were so faint that they could not follow David, whom they had made also to abide at the brook Besor: and they went forth to meet David, and to meet the people that were with him: and when David came near to the people, he saluted them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondensque omnis vir pessimus et iniquus de viris qui ierant cum David, dixit : Quia non venerunt nobiscum, non dabimus eis quidquam de præda quam eruimus : sed sufficiat unicuique uxor sua et filii : quos cum acceperint, recedant.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֜עַן כׇּל־אִֽישׁ־רָ֣ע וּבְלִיַּ֗עַל מֵהָאֲנָשִׁים֮ אֲשֶׁ֣ר הָלְכ֣וּ עִם־דָּוִד֒ וַיֹּאמְר֗וּ יַ֚עַן אֲשֶׁ֣ר לֹא־הָלְכ֣וּ עִמִּ֔י לֹא־נִתֵּ֣ן לָהֶ֔ם מֵהַשָּׁלָ֖ל אֲשֶׁ֣ר הִצַּ֑לְנוּ כִּֽי־אִם־אִ֤ישׁ אֶת־אִשְׁתּוֹ֙ וְאֶת־בָּנָ֔יו וְיִנְהֲג֖וּ וְיֵלֵֽכוּ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Then all the wicked and unjust men, that had gone with David, answering, said: Because they came not with us, we will not give them any thing of the prey which we have recovered: but let every man take his wife, and his children, and be contented with them, and go his way.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin vastasivat kaikki ne, jotka pahat ja tylyt miehet olivat niiden seasta, jotka Davidin kanssa olivat matkustaneet, ja sanoivat: ettei he menneet meidän kanssamme, ei pidä heille annettaman siitä saaliista jonka me saaneet olemme; mutta itsekukin ottakaan emäntänsä ja lapsensa ja menkään tiehensä.
verses.king_james: Then answered all the wicked men and men of Belial, of those that went with David, and said, Because they went not with us, we will not give them ought of the spoil that we have recovered, save to every man his wife and his children, that they may lead them away, and depart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem David : Non sic facietis, fratres mei, de his quæ tradidit nobis Dominus, et custodivit nos, et dedit latrunculos qui eruperant adversum nos, in manus nostras :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר דָּוִ֔ד לֹא־תַעֲשׂ֥וּ כֵ֖ן אֶחָ֑י אֵ֠ת אֲשֶׁר־נָתַ֨ן יְהֹוָ֥ה לָ֙נוּ֙ וַיִּשְׁמֹ֣ר אֹתָ֔נוּ וַיִּתֵּ֗ן אֶֽת־הַגְּד֛וּד הַבָּ֥א עָלֵ֖ינוּ בְּיָדֵֽנוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But David said: You shall not do so, my brethren, with these things, which the Lord hath given us, who hath kept us, and hath delivered the robbers that invaded us into our hands:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi David: ei teidän niin pidä tekemän, minun veljeni, sillä Herra on ne meille antanut, ja on meidät varjellut ja antanut sen sotaväen meidän käsiimme, joka tuli meitä vastaan.
verses.king_james: Then said David, Ye shall not do so, my brethren, with that which the Lord hath given us, who hath preserved us, and delivered the company that came against us into our hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: nec audiet vos quisquam super sermone hoc : æqua enim pars erit descendentis ad prælium, et remanentis ad sarcinas, et similiter divident.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמִי֙ יִשְׁמַ֣ע לָכֶ֔ם לַדָּבָ֖ר הַזֶּ֑ה כִּ֞י כְּחֵ֣לֶק ׀ הַיֹּרֵ֣ד בַּמִּלְחָמָ֗ה וּֽכְחֵ֛לֶק הַיֹּשֵׁ֥ב עַל־הַכֵּלִ֖ים יַחְדָּ֥ו יַחֲלֹֽקוּ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And no man shall hearken to you in this matter. But equal shall be the portion of him that went down to battle, and of him that abode at the baggage, and they shall divide alike.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuka teitä pitää kuuleman tässä asiassa? Senkaltainen kuin niiden osa on, jotka olivat sodassa meidän kanssamme, pitää myös oleman heidän osansa jotka kaluin tykönä ovat olleet, ja pitää yhdenkaltainen jako oleman.
verses.king_james: For who will hearken unto you in this matter? but as his part is that goeth down to the battle, so shall his part be that tarrieth by the stuff: they shall part alike.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est hoc ex die illa et deinceps, constitutum et præfinitum, et quasi lex in Israël usque in diem hanc.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֕י מֵהַיּ֥וֹם הַה֖וּא וָמָ֑עְלָה וַיְשִׂמֶ֜הָ לְחֹ֤ק וּלְמִשְׁפָּט֙ לְיִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל עַ֖ד הַיּ֥וֹם הַזֶּֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And this hath been done from that day forward, and since was made a statute and an ordinance, and as a law in Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tämä on ollut siitä ajasta ja aina sitte sääty ja oikeus Israelissa tähän päivään asti.
verses.king_james: And it was so from that day forward, that he made it a statute and an ordinance for Israel unto this day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit ergo David in Siceleg, et misit dona de præda senioribus Juda proximis suis, dicens : Accipite benedictionem de præda hostium Domini :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֤א דָוִד֙ אֶל־צִ֣קְלַ֔ג וַיְשַׁלַּ֧ח מֵהַשָּׁלָ֛ל לְזִקְנֵ֥י יְהוּדָ֖ה לְרֵעֵ֣הוּ לֵאמֹ֑ר הִנֵּ֤ה לָכֶם֙ בְּרָכָ֔ה מִשְּׁלַ֖ל אֹיְבֵ֥י יְהֹוָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Then David came to Siceleg, and sent presents of the prey to the ancients of Juda, his neighbours, saying: Receive a blessing of the prey of the enemies of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David tuli Ziglagiin ja lähetti saaliista ylimmäisille Juudasta, jotka hänen ystävänsä olivat, ja sanoi: katso, tässä on teille siunaus Herran vihollisten saaliista:
verses.king_james: And when David came to Ziklag, he sent of the spoil unto the elders of Judah, even to his friends, saying, Behold a present for you of the spoil of the enemies of the Lord;

verses.latin_vulgate: his qui erant in Bethel, et qui in Ramoth ad meridiem, et qui in Jether,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לַאֲשֶׁ֧ר בְּבֵֽית־אֵ֛ל {ס} וְלַאֲשֶׁ֥ר בְּרָמֽוֹת־נֶ֖גֶב {ס} וְלַאֲשֶׁ֥ר בְּיַתִּֽר׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: To them that were in Bethel, and that were in Ramoth to the south, and to them that were in Jether.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niille jotka olivat Betelissä, niille Ramotissa etelään päin, niille Jatirissa,
verses.king_james: To them which were in Beth–el, and to them which were in south Ramoth, and to them which were in Jattir,

verses.latin_vulgate: et qui in Aroër, et qui in Sephamoth, et qui in Esthamo,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלַאֲשֶׁ֧ר בַּעֲרֹעֵ֛ר {ס} וְלַאֲשֶׁ֥ר בְּשִֽׂפְמ֖וֹת {ס} וְלַאֲשֶׁ֥ר בְּאֶשְׁתְּמֹֽעַ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And to them that were in Aroer, and that were in Sephamoth, and that were in Esthamo,
verses.finnish_biblia: Niille Aroerissa, niille Siphmotissa, niille Estmoassa,
verses.king_james: And to them which were in Aroer, and to them which were in Siphmoth, and to them which were in Eshtemoa,

verses.latin_vulgate: et qui in Rachal, et qui in urbibus Jerameel, et qui in urbibus Ceni,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלַאֲשֶׁ֣ר בְּרָכָ֗ל {ס} וְלַֽאֲשֶׁר֙ בְּעָרֵ֣י הַיְּרַחְמְאֵלִ֔י {ס} וְלַאֲשֶׁ֖ר בְּעָרֵ֥י הַקֵּינִֽי׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And that were in Rachal, and that were in the cities of Jerameel, and that were in the cities of Ceni,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja niille kuin on Rakalissa, ja niille Jerakmelilaisten kaupungeista, ja niille Keniläisten kaupungeista,
verses.king_james: And to them which were in Rachal, and to them which were in the cities of the Jerahmeelites, and to them which were in the cities of the Kenites,

verses.latin_vulgate: et qui in Arama, et qui in lacu Asan, et qui in Athach,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלַאֲשֶׁ֧ר בְּחׇרְמָ֛ה {ס} וְלַאֲשֶׁ֥ר בְּבוֹר־עָשָׁ֖ן {ס} וְלַאֲשֶׁ֥ר בַּעֲתָֽךְ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And that were in Arama, and that were in the lake Asan, and that were in Athach,
verses.finnish_biblia: Niille Hormassa, ja niille Korasanissa, ja niille Atakissa,
verses.king_james: And to them which were in Hormah, and to them which were in Chor–ashan, and to them which were in Athach,

verses.latin_vulgate: et qui in Hebron, et reliquis qui erant in his locis in quibus commoratus fuerat David, ipse et viri ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלַאֲשֶׁ֖ר בְּחֶבְר֑וֹן וּֽלְכׇל־הַמְּקֹמ֛וֹת אֲשֶֽׁר־הִתְהַלֶּךְ־שָׁ֥ם דָּוִ֖ד ה֥וּא וַאֲנָשָֽׁיו׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And that were in Hebron, and to the rest that were in those places, in which David had abode with his men.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niille Hebronissa, ja kaikkiin paikkoihin, kussa David oli miehinensä vaeltanut.
verses.king_james: And to them which were in Hebron, and to all the places where David himself and his men were wont to haunt.
